text: **Anna:** Today at work, I am reading the {news|ニュース/報道|/njuːz/|Breaking news today.} for the first time.
**Anna:** I am really {nervous|緊張している/不安|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before a test.}
**Anna:** But my {boss|上司|/bɒs/|My boss is very kind.}, Miss Weaver, is here to help me.
**Caty:** Now, Anna, remember when we read the news, we are always reading {facts|事実|/fæks/|These are scientific facts.}
**Caty:** Okay, let's try the first story
**Anna:** Hello and welcome to The News.
**Anna:** A new book is very {popular|人気のある|/ˈpɒpjələr/|This song is very popular.} with children and families.
**Anna:** It is about a lost {duckling|子アヒル|/ˈdʌklɪŋ/|The cute duckling swam.}
**Anna:** The duck's mother cannot find him.
**Caty:** Anna, when you say the words duck and duckling, you look really {sad|悲しい|/sæd/|I feel sad today.}
**Anna:** Hello and welcome to The News.
**Anna:** A new book is very popular with children and families
**Anna:** It is about a lost duckling.
**Anna:** The duck's mother cannot find him, but a family gives him a {home|家/住み家|/hoʊm/|Welcome to our home.}
**Caty:** Anna, you are doing it {again|再び}!
**Caty:** I have an {idea|アイデア/考え}.
**Caty:** She's reading the second story and {go|進む/行く}.
**Anna:** Hello and {welcome|歓迎する/ようこそ|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to our show.}
**Anna:** In Indiana, a {grandmother|祖母} is the first 80-year-old woman to win the {Race|競争/レース} Car 500.
**Anna:** That is {awesome|素晴らしい/驚くべき}.
**Anna:** But it is {awesome|素晴らしい/驚くべき} that an eighty-year-old grandmother wins a car race.
**Anna:** Hello and welcome to the news.
**Anna:** In Indiana, a grandmother is the first 80-year-old woman to win the Race Car 500.
**Anna:** She {rarely|めったに〜ない} talks to {reporters|記者}, but when she does, she often says, Nothing can stop me now.
**Anna:** I am very happy for her.
**Caty:** Anna, you cannot say you are happy.
**Caty:** {Happy|幸せな} and {sad|悲しい} are feelings.
**Caty:** You can't have them in the news.
**Caty:** Okay, let's try the third story.
**Anna:** Hello and {welcome|ようこそ/歓迎|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to our show.} to the news.

**Anna:** City {politicians|政治家|/pəˈlɪtɪʃənz/|Politicians make important decisions.} in Big Town are using city money to have a big {party|パーティー/祝宴|/ˈpɑːrti/|Let's have a party tonight.} on a cruise ship
**Anna:** They are taking the money for the party from the children's {library|図書館|/ˈlaɪbreri/|I love reading books in the library.}
**Anna:** That makes me very {angry|怒った/激怒|/ˈæŋɡri/|He was angry about the mistake.}
**Caty:** Anna, you cannot say you are angry.
**Anna:** What can I do, Miss Weaver?
**Anna:** Take out my {feelings|感情/気持ち|/ˈfiːlɪŋz/|My feelings are complicated.} and put them here on the news desk?
**Anna:** This is going to be a very {long|長い/長期の|/lɔːŋ/|We had a long conversation.} day.
